description: Trigger-posture suite for the issue-normalization skill. It checks whether the skill activates for issue intake and issue drafting, preserves source fidelity, stays out of clarify and brainstorming territory, and avoids drifting into prioritization or implementation planning.

assertion_definitions.in_scope_intake_detected: The response recognizes that the task is issue intake or issue drafting rather than generic implementation work.
assertion_definitions.source_preserved: The response keeps a provenance note, source pointer, or equivalent reference to where the issue came from.
assertion_definitions.canonical_draft_shape: The result takes the shape of a normalized issue draft, not a plan, implementation patch, or generic advice list.
assertion_definitions.missing_info_explicit: When the source is incomplete, the response names the missing information instead of inventing certainty.
assertion_definitions.next_workflow_named: The response ends with a clear next workflow, owner, or routing recommendation.
assertion_definitions.boundary_respected: Adjacent tasks such as brainstorming, clarify, review-only, or implementation planning are not incorrectly handled as issue normalization.
assertion_definitions.avoids_prioritization_drift: The response does not silently convert issue intake into backlog ranking, scheduling, or budget decisions.

cases.name: Turn a messy bug report into a normalized issue
cases.prompt: Turn these Slack notes into a bug issue: 'Import still fails for some CSVs after yesterday's fix. Ops saw two stack traces mentioning row parsing, but I only have one screenshot and no repro yet. It seems to block the finance team every few days.'

in_scope_intake_detected.check: The response should recognize this as issue intake work.

source_preserved.check: The result should keep that the source was Slack notes and partial ops evidence.

canonical_draft_shape.check: The output should resemble a normalized issue draft with problem, evidence, and acceptance criteria.

missing_info_explicit.check: The response should explicitly record missing repro steps or incomplete evidence.

cases.name: Convert review findings into a follow-up issue
cases.prompt: Normalize this reviewer note into a follow-up issue: 'The auth middleware still trusts the legacy header path during fallback. We should track removing that before GA, but not block this PR.'

in_scope_intake_detected.check: The response should recognize the task as follow-up issue drafting.

source_preserved.check: The draft should note that the issue came from a review finding.

next_workflow_named.check: The response should recommend the next workflow or owner for the follow-up issue.

cases.name: Implementation ambiguity should route to clarify instead
cases.prompt: Before building the RBAC feature, clarify whether roles should live in JWT claims or the database.

boundary_respected.check: The response should identify this as clarification for a chosen task, not issue-normalization.

cases.name: Open-ended direction choice should route away
cases.prompt: Help me think through whether we should spin our internal automation into a standalone SaaS product.

boundary_respected.check: The response should route this to brainstorming or equivalent direction-setting work.

cases.name: Backlog ranking should not trigger normalization
cases.prompt: Rank these ten backlog items and tell me what we should do this sprint.

boundary_respected.check: The response should not launch issue intake work.

avoids_prioritization_drift.check: The response should recognize prioritization as a different workflow rather than pretending issue normalization solves it.

runner_instructions.setup: Make the issue-normalization skill available on the surface under test. Include neighboring skills such as clarify, brainstorming, and review when checking boundary behavior.
runner_instructions.execution: Run each case as a real user request. Capture the response text and any normalized issue artifact that is produced.
runner_instructions.evaluation: Score only the listed assertions for each case. Pay special attention to whether the response preserves provenance and avoids drifting into prioritization or implementation planning.
